metadata.notes: agy has an AskQuestion tool, but the filtered transcript records only status:DONE steps — the WAITING/blocking state is never persisted (no STEP_STATUS_WAITING line in either transcript), and the adapter has no user-blocking/PermissionPending signal. daemon=incapable → frozen.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
daemon_capability: incapable
driver_capability: ready (n/a — frozen)

Route: frozen (irrlicht fundamentally cannot observe the `waiting` episode for antigravity).

## What the scenario pins

`ready → working → waiting`; the `waiting` episode must be USER-VISIBLE (no skip-over); after the user replies, `waiting → working → ready`. Final state: `waiting`.

## agent_supports: partial

The agy binary ships an AskQuestion tool (binary strings: `AskQuestion`, `askQuestion`, `ask_question`, and the cancel path `AskQuestion cancelled by user`), so the agent CAN pose a blocking question. `partial` (not `yes`) because the blocking/pause is a transient UI/IPC state, not a durable observable feature surface.

## daemon_capability: incapable

This is the load-bearing finding. The Antigravity adapter is transcript-first and the parser has NO user-blocking / PermissionPending / waiting signal — the state model is purely USER_INPUT→working, PLANNER_RESPONSE(+tool_calls)→working, terminal no-tool PLANNER_RESPONSE→ready. There is no path that emits `waiting`.

More fundamentally, the blocking state is never PERSISTED to the file the daemon tails. The binary defines a full step-status enum (STEP_STATUS_WAITING, STEP_STATUS_PENDING, STEP_STATUS_GENERATING, STEP_STATUS_INTERRUPTED, …) but these are internal IPC states: every line in BOTH the filtered transcript.jsonl AND the unfiltered transcript_full.jsonl is status:"DONE" (verified across all real conversations). An in-flight AskQuestion is an in-progress (non-DONE) state that is only appended once resolved — so a blocking question leaves no `waiting` trace for any parser to read. No trace exists → the 3-state model cannot represent the episode for this agent.

## driver_capability: n/a

Moot under a frozen route.
caveats: Re-assess if Antigravity ever persists non-DONE (WAITING/PENDING) steps to the transcript, or if the adapter gains a user-blocking-tool signal sourced from outside the transcript (e.g. the SQLite store).

sources.note: no waiting/PermissionPending/user-blocking emission path; state model is working/ready only
100% status:"DONE" even in the unfiltered view — in-progress/blocking states are never persisted
binary strings: AskQuestion tool exists (+ 'AskQuestion cancelled by user'), and a STEP_STATUS_WAITING enum value exists — but it is an internal state, never written to the transcript
